comment.body: What I see, perhaps obvious to everyone, is that hyperscalers evaluation is still anchored in the past Capex light model while these companies have become Capex heavy. Also, the software industry seems to face headwind, the sustainability of growth and margin is being questioned, suppressing the valuation. Now if hyperscalers and softwares are in trouble, then hardwares will be questioned as well… Other than that I am not sure if there are paired trades like long SMH short IGV/BTC…

comment.body: I'm not speaking for you. I think there is a great deal of ignorance about China in the West. We can all name five cities in the US, but if the question is about China, I think the number of people capable of answering it drops dramatically. We can't name a few cities, but we think we know how they think? The US is the one that would undoubtedly have invaded Taiwan. It is the US that needs to control Taiwan. China already controls Taiwan's chips de facto, which is the problem for the US and why it needs conflict. On the other hand, China's view of Taiwan as one of its provinces is an emotional issue, and this is true with or without chips.

comment.body: Yes, the US notably "betrayed" Taiwan (the Free China) in the 70s when it recognized the Red China. Politicians (Nixon or Carter or Clinton or Trump) err all the time, unfortunately; in the end one has to find a balance between idealism and realism... Any country, be it Ukraine or Taiwan or South Korea or Poland etc., if it wants to prosper, regardless of how the superpowers treat it, should primarily focus on itself; starting from having good hearts, good institution etc., then it will have a good chance.
